qa_pairs.question: When a system undergoes a complete data centre shutdown as part of renewal activities, will users' files be deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: No, users' files will not be deleted. During renewal activities, each National Host Site will migrate /project and /home data from the existing storage system to the new one. Tape systems for backups and /nearline data are not being replaced, so they will remain unchanged.

qa_pairs.question: Will existing jobs and applications be compatible with the new Advanced Research Computing systems after the infrastructure upgrade?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally, existing jobs and applications will be able to run, but the new CPUs and GPUs may require recompilation or reconfiguration of some applications. The standard software environment will be available, and commercial software licenses are planned to be transitioned to ensure identical access for users, though there is a small risk that software providers may change their licensing terms.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find information regarding the schedule for system outages during the installation and transition to the new Advanced Research Computing infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Each National Host Site will have its own schedule for outages. Specific outages will be described on the system status web page (status.alliancecan.ca). More general updates will be provided on the wiki page, and users will also receive periodic emails with updates and outage notices.
